title: Earnings Call: Aterian Reports Improved Margins, Anticipates Profitability
text: Aterian Margin Improvement and Profitability Trend Analysis. Recent Margin Improvements: In Q3 2025, Aterian's contribution margin improved from 7.8% in Q2 to over 15% in Q3, and the company's adjusted EBITDA loss narrowed to just over $400,000 from a loss of $2.2 million in Q2. Q3 2025 contribution margin improved by over 700 basis points from Q2 2025, back to over 15%. Historical Trend: The company has shown significant margin improvement over a longer timeframe. Full-year 2024 results showed net revenue of $99.0M (down from $142.6M in 2023), with enhanced gross margin of 62.1% (vs 49.3%) and significantly narrowed operating loss of $11.8M (vs $76.2M). Key Drivers and Challenges: Management attributed sharp year-over-year declines in revenue and gross margin to tariff-related pricing actions and broader demand softness, but reported that key profitability metrics improved sequentially through aggressive cost containment. Over time, the contribution margin of consumable products will improve the company's overall profitability. The company believes top-line growth is its biggest challenge, and is committed to addressing it thoughtfully and profitably. In summary, Aterian has demonstrated impressive sequential margin improvement through cost reduction and operational efficiency, though the company continues to face revenue headwinds from tariffs and softer demand.
